user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: The Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook, damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. The Plaintiff claims loss of goodwill and mental agony, seeking Rs. 10,00,000 in damages and a permanent injunction against further publication.

slots.value: Facebook or any other social media platform

missing_fields: Date of publication (cause of action date)
Plaintiff and Defendant names and addresses
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the specified District Court
Defamatory content is accessible to the public on Facebook

(b)Facts not yet material to a case. The pleadings should only contain such facts as are material at the 
present stage of the suit. It is not proper to anticipate the answer of the adversary. To do so is like leaping 
before one comes to the stile. 34 Similarly, a defendant should not plead to any matter which is not alleged 
against him. Thus, if a defendant is sued for slander, the only way to meet the plaintiffs claim is to plead (1) 
that the words in question were not spoken and published, or (2) that they were spoken and published and 
are true, or (3) that they were spoken and published and are privileged. If, instead of so doing, he pleads 
that he did not say the words alleged in the plaint, but that he said something else; and that the something 
else which he said was true and spoken on a privileged occasion, the defence will be struck out under r 16 
below, as one embarrassing the fair trial of the suit.35

draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing/Carrying on business at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
Within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.                              ...PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing/Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.                    ...DEFENDANT

PLAINT UNDER THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 SEEKING (I) DAMAGES/COMPENSATION FOR DEFAMATION AMOUNTING TO RS. 10,00,000/- AND (II) A DECREE OF PERMANENT INJUNCTION RESTRAINING FURTHER PUBLICATION ON FACEBOOK OR ANY OTHER SOCIAL MEDIA PLATFORM

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Parties
   1.1 The Plaintiff is an individual/business entity engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} and has built reputation and goodwill in the course of its business.
   1.2 The Defendant is an individual who maintains/uses an account on Facebook and has published content concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook, as pleaded herein.

2. Jurisdiction
   2.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within {{JURISDICTION_FACTS_FOR_TERRITORY}} and the impugned publication was accessed/read by third persons within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.
   2.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the Plaintiff values the present suit at Rs. 10,00,000/- for the relief of damages/compensation and seeks reliefs within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Facts Constituting Cause of Action

placeholders_used.reason: Draft cited Article 75 Limitation Act without any supporting provision text in provided RAG_CHUNKS; correct limitation provision must be verified before filing.
COURT_FEE_CONTEXT did not provide jurisdiction-specific rates/fee schedule for injunction relief; must be verified as per applicable State Court Fees Act/Rules.

blocking_issues.issue: Statutory reference cited without RAG support — may be sourced from training memory which can produce incorrect provision numbers for this cause of action type
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the unsupported citation with a placeholder (e.g., {{LIMITATION_PROVISION}}) and record the reason; verify the correct provision before filing
blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 9.1 (Limitation): citation to “Article 75 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act (… compensation for libel) … one year”

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action anchored to a procedural step rather than the date when the right to sue actually accrued under the applicable law
blocking_issues.fix: Identify the correct accrual date from FACTS based on the cause of action type (date of first publication/knowledge of publication), and use that as the cause of action date; procedural steps are not accrual events. If the date is not available in FACTS, use a placeholder consistently (e.g., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}) and align limitation and cause-of-action paragraphs to that date.
blocking_issues.location: Paragraphs 9.2, 9.3 and 10.2: accrual date pleaded as {{RIGHT_TO_SUE_ACCRUAL_DATE}} (not tied consistently to the pleaded publication date)

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee amount stated without verified rate × value computation; confirm whether applicable fee schedule is ad valorem or fixed for this doc_type and jurisdiction
blocking_issues.fix: Compute fee per the applicable fee schedule (rate × suit value) or use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder if the rate is not confirmed in COURT_FEE_CONTEXT. Also clarify bifurcated court-fee treatment for damages (ad valorem) and injunction (fixed/nominal) as per the applicable State Court Fees Act.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Document reference label mismatch risk: body references screenshots but does not explicitly refer to Annexure label used in the List of Documents
non_blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit in-body references using the same labels as the List of Documents (e.g., “Annexure P-1”) wherever the screenshots/notice/proof are mentioned, or revise the List of Documents to match body references exactly.
non_blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 4.1 (mentions “screenshots”) and List of Documents/Enclosures (Annexure P-1 etc.)

non_blocking_issues.issue: Verification clause is not in standard CPC verification form and refers to ‘Prayer’ being true; also contains unresolved placeholder for paragraph range
non_blocking_issues.fix: Use a standard verification: specify which paragraphs are true to knowledge and which are based on information believed to be true; replace {{PARA_COUNT_BEFORE_PRAYER}} with the correct last paragraph number and avoid verifying the ‘Prayer’ as facts.

unsupported_statements.statement: “This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within {{JURISDICTION_FACTS_FOR_TERRITORY}} and the impugned publication was accessed/read by third persons within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.”
unsupported_statements.reason: No territorial jurisdiction facts (place of publication/access, parties’ residence/business, or where reputation/business affected) are provided in FACTS/JURISDICTION.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The said publication was made publicly and/or was visible to the Defendant’s friends/followers and was read/seen by third parties, including the Plaintiff’s customers…”
unsupported_statements.reason: No supporting particulars (privacy settings, names/identities of third-party viewers, or any specific instances of customers seeing it) are provided in FACTS/EVIDENCE.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The Defendant’s statements were made with the intent to harm…”
unsupported_statements.reason: Malice/intent is pleaded but FACTS provide no specific basis (prior disputes, motive, prior threats/messages) beyond conclusory assertion.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The Defendant failed to verify any facts…”
unsupported_statements.reason: No facts are provided showing what verification was possible/expected or any conduct evidencing reckless disregard.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The publication was capable of being shared… thereby aggravating the defamation and the damages caused.”
unsupported_statements.reason: General platform capability is true in the abstract, but no facts are provided that the post was actually shared/republished or that virality occurred.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The cause of action further arose and continues to arise on each day the impugned post(s) remain accessible on Facebook and/or are further shared/republished…”
unsupported_statements.reason: Continuing accessibility/continued publication is not supported by any provided facts (no statement that the post remains online, dates of continued viewing, or repeated publications).
No facts provided regarding any notice/demand/takedown request.
No information provided on other proceedings.
